title: Using OPeNDAP-enabled Applications to Access Australian Data Services
author: Pugh, Tim F.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: These slides were presented at the eResearch 2011 meeting in Melbourne, Australia. The workshop covered both using a web browser as an OPeNDAP client as well as application-clients like Matlab and other tolls. Also covered are some issues relating to clients like configuration of the .dodsrc file to enable certificate-based authentication. This slide deck is one of a total of four sets of slides (all uploaded to Zenodo) that were used for this 1/2 day workshop. Summary: The workshop offers participants a hands-on experience with the latest OPeNDAP server systems, Hyrax and THREDDS, used in Australian research sites. Attendees will engage in four segments focusing on discovering data services and products, exploring use cases of OPeNDAP data services and client applications, understanding metadata discovery and data access, and accessing geospatial and aggregation data services. The workshop aims to gather feedback on the usefulness of the services and tools and foster discussions on future developments. Sessions include tutorials on accessing data via web browsers and OPeNDAP-enabled tools, understanding OPeNDAP service protocols, and exploring metadata responses and data extraction. The broader vision of OPeNDAP is to establish a unified data access protocol, while its fundamental objective is to facilitate internet access to scientific data by providing a protocol for data access, hiding data format complexities, and offering subsetting capabilities. The workshop also covers essential definitions, syntax, and responses of OPeNDAP and THREDDS services, along with tutorials on exploring catalogs, deciphering URLs, and accessing different types of DAP responses. A list of OPeNDAP servers is provided for participants' reference.
